skill.name: scoop-ui-refactor
skill.description: Guides project-specific UI refactors for the Scoop news platform, including layouts, globe UX, research workspace, feed scrolling, and no-emoji cleanup.
skill.summary: Refactor Scoop frontend UI while preserving existing product behavior.
skill.author: BenderFendor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found the weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts to be false positives from normal prose, markdown paths, and UI workflow text. The only meaningful risk is a legitimate local ripgrep instruction and broad project-specific editing guidance, with no evidence of malicious intent, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Command Guidance
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to use ripgrep to find emoji usage during a UI cleanup. This is a legitimate local search command, but it still requires normal user approval and scope control because it may inspect project files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and limited to local search for emoji usage. No shell metacharacters, remote endpoints, credential access, or dynamic user-controlled command construction are present.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings are not supported by the SKILL.md context. The referenced lines contain normal project description, UI requirements, and verification text rather than cryptographic code or host reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were manually reviewed and contain no cryptographic algorithm usage or reconnaissance commands. The alerts appear to be substring matches inside ordinary natural-language content.

content.value_statement: Scoop UI changes can regress feed, globe, research, and modal behavior. This skill gives a focused refactor workflow that preserves features while improving density and navigation.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the main Scoop frontend files to review before UI changes.
Defines requirements for compact home layout and feed controls.
Guides globe UX updates for selected countries, source counts, and reset actions.
Specifies research workspace changes, including query bar, modes, and sidebar layout.
Requires checks for view modes, modals, filters, research results, and snap scrolling.
Directs removal of emoji usage from UI strings, logs, and documentation.
limitations: It is tailored to a specific Scoop project structure and may not fit other repositories.
It does not include executable scripts or automated tests.
It assumes the user can inspect and edit frontend files manually with an AI assistant.
It does not resolve existing lint errors that are unrelated to the UI refactor.

use_cases.title: Compact News Feed Refactor
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to reduce empty space while keeping filters, view modes, and article actions intact.

use_cases.title: Globe Selection UX Cleanup
use_cases.description: Improve selected-country feedback, reset controls, and source summaries without removing existing globe behavior.

use_cases.title: Research Workspace Refresh
use_cases.description: Restructure query, history, and result views while preserving embedded sources and research output rendering.

prompt_templates.title: Review Scoop UI Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the scoop-ui-refactor skill to review the Scoop frontend surfaces. Summarize the required UI changes and the files I should inspect first.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Home Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the scoop-ui-refactor skill to compact the Scoop home page layout. Preserve view modes, filters, modals, bookmarks, likes, and favorites.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Globe And Research UX
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the scoop-ui-refactor skill to improve the globe selection flow and research workspace. Keep all existing data flows and source rendering working.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Snap Scroll Regression Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the scoop-ui-refactor skill to inspect the feed snap-scroll implementation. Verify wheel, touch, and arrow-key behavior while keeping the global header accessible.

output_examples.input: Refactor the Scoop home page for higher density.
output: A concise change plan for the home layout and feed controls.
A list of existing behaviors that must be preserved during edits.
A verification checklist for view switching, filters, and article actions.

output_examples.input: Improve the globe view and remove emoji usage.
output: Updated UX requirements for selected-country state and reset behavior.
A cleanup checklist for replacing flag emojis with labels or icons.
Manual checks for source counts and top-source display.

output_examples.input: Check whether the feed snap-scroll design is safe.
output: A review of section sizing, scroll container behavior, and header accessibility.
Test steps for wheel, touch, and keyboard navigation.
Notes about unrelated lint failures that should not block layout validation.

best_practices: Start by reviewing the listed Scoop surfaces before making visual changes.
Verify preserved behavior after each layout or scrolling update.
Replace emoji with text labels or icons across UI, logs, and documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not remove existing feed actions while changing the article layout.
Do not make full-screen sections that hide titles or block access to the header.
Do not apply this workflow blindly to repositories with a different structure.

faq.question: What project is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the Scoop news platform frontend described in the skill file.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance. Any local command, such as ripgrep, must be run by the assistant or user in context.

faq.question: Can it help with snap-scroll feed behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes sizing, snap container behavior, input handling, and verification checks for feed scrolling.

faq.question: Does it include automated tests?
faq.answer: No. It lists manual checks for core UI behavior and expects the project test tools to be used separately.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The skill is project-specific and includes local search guidance, so users should confirm repository scope before applying edits.
